params.name: 100 Years of Solitude
params.description:
On Aug 5, 1982 AD, Colombian author Gabriel Garcia Marquez was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature for 100 Years of Solitude.
100 Years of Solitude tells the legendary story of the Buendia family through seven generations, depicting the rise and fall of the town of Macondo in the Caribbean. It incorporates mythical legends, folk stories, religious allusions, and other mysterious elements to create a magical and realistic world, which is recognized as the most representative work of magical …
